forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRRN2M0E5YXA9E24N1BM2BVP
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8480'
+project: ai-panel
+title: ACP agent process manager + model enumeration (kanban-app backend)
+---
+## What
+The kanban-app Tauri backend manages the ACP agent process lifecycle — and nothing else about ACP. It is NOT in the ACP data path: it never pipes ACP bytes and never adapts the protocol through Tauri events. Its entire job is "spawn the agent, hand the webview a `ws://` URL, kill it later."
+
+- New `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_process.rs`.
+- Detect Claude Code: `claude` resolvable on `PATH` (honor a `CLAUDE_CLI` override).
+- Enumerate selectable models: a Claude Code entry (gated on detection — present-but-disabled with a hint when absent) plus configured local models from `swissarmyhammer-config`.
+- For a chosen model, spawn the WebSocket-mode ACP agent — `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` with the model's config (Claude Code vs local llama) — and capture the bound `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL.
+- Resolve the `sah` agent binary: bundle it as a Tauri sidecar like the existing `kanban` sidecar, or require it on `PATH`. Resolve and document; reuse `cli_install::resolve_bundled_cli`-style logic. Also resolve the bundled `kanban` path for the webview to build the `mcpServers` entry.
+- Tauri commands (register in `main.rs`): `ai_list_models() -> Vec<Model>` (`{ id, label, kind, available, hint }`); `ai_start_agent(model_id) -> { wsUrl, kanbanCommand }`; `ai_stop_agent(handle)`. `ai_start_agent` returns only the ws URL plus the resolved `kanban` command — a one-time config handoff, not a data channel.
+- Track agent child processes in `AppState`; kill them on window/board/app teardown.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `claude` detection works (`Some` when on `PATH`/`CLAUDE_CLI`, else `None`).
+- [ ] `ai_list_models` returns a Claude Code entry with `available` reflecting detection, plus configured local models.
+- [ ] `ai_start_agent` spawns the WebSocket-mode agent and returns a `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL that a WebSocket can connect to.
+- [ ] Agent processes are killed on teardown — no orphans.
+- [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests: `claude` detection against a fake binary on `PATH`; model enumeration shape.
+- [ ] Integration test: `ai_start_agent` returns a URL a WebSocket client can connect to and `initialize` over; `ai_stop_agent` (and teardown) kills the process.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the detection and `ai_start_agent` round-trip tests first.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,29 +6,28 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8480'
 project: ai-panel
-title: ACP agent process manager + model enumeration (kanban-app backend)
+title: Model selection and the AI agent endpoint command surface
 ---
 ## What
-The kanban-app Tauri backend manages the ACP agent process lifecycle — and nothing else about ACP. It is NOT in the ACP data path: it never pipes ACP bytes and never adapts the protocol through Tauri events. Its entire job is "spawn the agent, hand the webview a `ws://` URL, kill it later."
+Wire model choice to the in-process ACP agent (the "WebSocket ACP agent" task) and give the webview the commands it needs.

-- New `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/agent_process.rs`.
+- New `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs`.
 - Detect Claude Code: `claude` resolvable on `PATH` (honor a `CLAUDE_CLI` override).
-- Enumerate selectable models: a Claude Code entry (gated on detection — present-but-disabled with a hint when absent) plus configured local models from `swissarmyhammer-config`.
-- For a chosen model, spawn the WebSocket-mode ACP agent — `sah agent acp --listen 127.0.0.1:0` with the model's config (Claude Code vs local llama) — and capture the bound `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL.
-- Resolve the `sah` agent binary: bundle it as a Tauri sidecar like the existing `kanban` sidecar, or require it on `PATH`. Resolve and document; reuse `cli_install::resolve_bundled_cli`-style logic. Also resolve the bundled `kanban` path for the webview to build the `mcpServers` entry.
-- Tauri commands (register in `main.rs`): `ai_list_models() -> Vec<Model>` (`{ id, label, kind, available, hint }`); `ai_start_agent(model_id) -> { wsUrl, kanbanCommand }`; `ai_stop_agent(handle)`. `ai_start_agent` returns only the ws URL plus the resolved `kanban` command — a one-time config handoff, not a data channel.
-- Track agent child processes in `AppState`; kill them on window/board/app teardown.
+- Enumerate selectable models: a Claude Code entry (gated on detection — present-but-disabled with a hint when absent) plus configured local llama models from `swissarmyhammer-config` (only when the `ai-local-models` Cargo feature is built).
+- Tie selection to the in-process agent: when the webview picks a model, the backend prepares the in-process agent endpoint for that `ModelConfig` and yields the `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL the TypeScript client connects to.
+- Tauri commands (register in `main.rs`): `ai_list_models() -> Vec<Model>` (`{ id, label, kind, available, hint }`); `ai_start_agent(model_id) -> { wsUrl, kanbanCommand }` — a one-time config handoff: the loopback `ws://` URL plus the resolved bundled `kanban` command for the TS client's `newSession.mcpServers` entry. This is config discovery, NOT a data channel.
+- Lifecycle: stop the in-process agent and its WebSocket server on window/board/app teardown; track in `AppState`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `claude` detection works (`Some` when on `PATH`/`CLAUDE_CLI`, else `None`).
-- [ ] `ai_list_models` returns a Claude Code entry with `available` reflecting detection, plus configured local models.
-- [ ] `ai_start_agent` spawns the WebSocket-mode agent and returns a `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL that a WebSocket can connect to.
-- [ ] Agent processes are killed on teardown — no orphans.
+- [ ] `claude` detection returns `Some` when on `PATH`/`CLAUDE_CLI`, else `None`.
+- [ ] `ai_list_models` returns a Claude Code entry with `available` reflecting detection, plus local llama models when built with `ai-local-models`.
+- [ ] `ai_start_agent` returns a `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL a WebSocket can `initialize` over for the chosen model, plus the resolved `kanban` command.
+- [ ] Teardown stops the in-process agent and WS server — no leaks.
 - [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests: `claude` detection against a fake binary on `PATH`; model enumeration shape.
-- [ ] Integration test: `ai_start_agent` returns a URL a WebSocket client can connect to and `initialize` over; `ai_stop_agent` (and teardown) kills the process.
+- [ ] Unit tests: `claude` detection against a fake binary on `PATH`; model enumeration shape (with/without `ai-local-models`).
+- [ ] Integration test: `ai_start_agent` returns a URL a WebSocket client connects to and `initialize`s over for the selected model; teardown stops it.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow

+Wire model choice to the in-process ACP agent (the "WebSocket ACP agent" task) and give the webview the two endpoints it needs.

 - New `apps/kanban-app/src/ai/models.rs`.
 - Detect Claude Code: `claude` resolvable on `PATH` (honor a `CLAUDE_CLI` override).
 - Enumerate selectable models: a Claude Code entry (gated on detection — present-but-disabled with a hint when absent) plus configured local llama models from `swissarmyhammer-config` (only when the `ai-local-models` Cargo feature is built).
-- Tie selection to the in-process agent: when the webview picks a model, the backend prepares the in-process agent endpoint for that `ModelConfig` and yields the `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL the TypeScript client connects to.
-- Tauri commands (register in `main.rs`): `ai_list_models() -> Vec<Model>` (`{ id, label, kind, available, hint }`); `ai_start_agent(model_id) -> { wsUrl, kanbanCommand }` — a one-time config handoff: the loopback `ws://` URL plus the resolved bundled `kanban` command for the TS client's `newSession.mcpServers` entry. This is config discovery, NOT a data channel.
+- Tie selection to the in-process agent: when the webview picks a model, the backend prepares the in-process agent endpoint for that `ModelConfig` and yields the `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL.
+- Tauri commands (register in `main.rs`): `ai_list_models() -> Vec<Model>` (`{ id, label, kind, available, hint }`); `ai_start_agent(model_id, board_path) -> { wsUrl, mcpUrl }` — a one-time config handoff: the loopback `ws://` agent URL plus the board's full-SAH-toolset MCP URL (`http://127.0.0.1:<port>/mcp` from the per-board toolset server). The TypeScript client puts `mcpUrl` in `newSession.mcpServers`. This is config discovery, NOT a data channel.
 - Lifecycle: stop the in-process agent and its WebSocket server on window/board/app teardown; track in `AppState`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `claude` detection returns `Some` when on `PATH`/`CLAUDE_CLI`, else `None`.
 - [ ] `ai_list_models` returns a Claude Code entry with `available` reflecting detection, plus local llama models when built with `ai-local-models`.
-- [ ] `ai_start_agent` returns a `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL a WebSocket can `initialize` over for the chosen model, plus the resolved `kanban` command.
+- [ ] `ai_start_agent` returns a `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>` URL a WebSocket can `initialize` over for the chosen model, plus the board's full-SAH-toolset `mcpUrl`.
 - [ ] Teardown stops the in-process agent and WS server — no leaks.
 - [ ] `cargo build -p kanban-app` is clean.

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Unit tests: `claude` detection against a fake binary on `PATH`; model enumeration shape (with/without `ai-local-models`).
-- [ ] Integration test: `ai_start_agent` returns a URL a WebSocket client connects to and `initialize`s over for the selected model; teardown stops it.
+- [ ] Integration test: `ai_start_agent` returns a `wsUrl` a WebSocket client `initialize`s over for the selected model, and an `mcpUrl` that resolves to the board's SAH toolset; teardown stops it.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p kanban-app` is green.

 ## Workflow
